Subprefecture|en|Hidaka Subprefecture|WD=Q1137954|lt=Hidaka|1=Hidaka Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|日高振興局}} |{{ill|Urakawa, Hokkaido|en|Urakawa, Hokkaido|WD=Q1355506|lt=Urakawa|1=Urakawa, Hokkaido}} |{{ill|Shinhidaka, Hokkaido|en|Shinhidaka, Hokkaido|WD=Q1210679|lt=Shinhidaka|1=Shinhidaka, Hokkaido}} | align="right" |76,084 | align="right" |4,811.97 |15.81 | 0|| 7 || 0 |- !4 |'''{{ill|Oshima Subprefecture|en|Oshima Subprefecture|WD=Q2226825|lt=Oshima|1=Oshima Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|渡島総合振興局}} |[[:en:Hakodate, Hokkaido|Hakodate]] |Hakodate | align="right" |433,475 | align="right" |3,936.46 |110.12 |2 || 9 || 0 |- !c |↳ '''{{ill|Hiyama Subprefecture|en|Hiyama Subprefecture|WD=Q1142537|lt=Hiyama|1=Hiyama Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|檜山振興局}} |{{ill|Esashi, Hokkaido (Hiyama)|en|Esashi, Hokkaido (Hiyama)|WD=Q1348738|lt=Esashi|1=Esashi, Hokkaido (Hiyama)}} |{{ill|Setana, Hokkaido|en|Setana, Hokkaido|WD=Q1036505|lt=Setana|1=Setana, Hokkaido}} | align="right" |43,210 | align="right" |2,629.94 |16.43 | 0|| 7 ||0 |- !5 |'''{{ill|Kamikawa Subprefecture|en|Kamikawa Subprefecture|WD=Q1152333|lt=Kamikawa|1=Kamikawa Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|上川総合振興局}} |{{ill|Asahikawa|en|Asahikawa|WD=Q200740|lt=Asahikawa|1=Asahikawa}} |Asahikawa | align="right" |527,575 | align="right" |10,619.20 |49.68 |4 || 17 || 2 |- !d |↳ '''{{ill|Rumoi Subprefecture|en|Rumoi Subprefecture|WD=Q1151453|lt=Rumoi|1=Rumoi Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|留萌振興局}} |{{ill|Rumoi, Hokkaido|en|Rumoi, Hokkaido|WD=Q714628|lt=Rumoi|1=Rumoi, Hokkaido}} |Rumoi | align="right" |53,916 | align="right" |3,445.75 |15.65 |1 || 6 || 1 |- !6 |'''{{ill|Sōya Subprefecture|en|Sōya Subprefecture|WD=Q934821|lt=Sōya|1=Sōya Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|宗谷総合振興局}} |[[:en:Wakkanai, Hokkaido|Wakkanai]] |Wakkanai | align="right" |71,423 | align="right" |4,625.09 |15.44 |1 || 8 || 1 |- !7 |'''{{ill|Okhotsk Subprefecture|en|Okhotsk Subprefecture|WD=Q1054546|lt=Okhotsk|1=Okhotsk Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|オホーツク総合振興局}} |{{ill|Abashiri|en|Abashiri|WD=Q305640|lt=Abashiri|1=Abashiri}} |[[:en:Kitami, Hokkaido|Kitami]] | align="right" |309,487 | align="right" |10,690.62 |28.95 |3 || 14 || 1 |- !8 |'''{{ill|Tokachi Subprefecture|en|Tokachi Subprefecture|WD=Q1137454|lt=Tokachi|1=Tokachi Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|十勝総合振興局}} |[[:en:Obihiro, Hokkaido|Obihiro]] |Obihiro | align="right" |353,291 | align="right" |10,831.24 |32.62 |1 || 16 || 2 |- !9 |'''{{ill|Kushiro Subprefecture|en|Kushiro Subprefecture|WD=Q1141563|lt=Kushiro|1=Kushiro Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|釧路総合振興局}} |[[:en:Kushiro, Hokkaido|Kushiro]] |Kushiro | align="right" |252,571 | align="right" |5,997.38 |42.11 |1 || 6 || 1 |- !e |↳ '''{{ill|Nemuro Subprefecture|en|Nemuro Subprefecture|WD=Q1151431|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro Subprefecture}}''' |{{lang|ja|根室振興局}} |{{ill|Nemuro, Hokkaido|en|Nemuro, Hokkaido|WD=Q502205|lt=Nemuro|1=Nemuro, Hokkaido}} |Nemuro | align="right" |84,035 | align="right" |3,406.23 |24.67 |1 || 4 || {{center|*}} |- | colspan="11" |* Japan claims the southern part of {{ill|Kuril Islands|en|Kuril Islands|WD=Q80389|lt=Kuril Islands|1=Kuril Islands}} ({{ill|Kuril Islands dispute|en|Kuril Islands dispute|WD=Q389717|lt=Northern Territories|1=Kuril Islands dispute}}), currently administered by Russia,
belong to Nemuro Subprefecture divided into six villages. However, the table above excludes these islands' data. |} ==Reorganisation== The prefectural government of Hokkaido planned to reorganise the current fourteen subprefectures into nine sub-prefectural bureaus. Five subprefectures, namely Hidaka, Hiyama, Ishikari, Nemuro, and Rumoi were subject to be cut down. The capital municipalities of these subprefectures opposed the plan, but on June 28, 2008, the prefectural council passed the ordinance of the reorganization.{{in lang|ja}} [http://www.namara-hokkaido.net/topics/news/news.php?id=15017 釧路支庁が道東総合振興局に、道の支庁改革条例成立] (Kushiro Subprefecture will be Dōtō General Development Bureau, the ordinance for the sub-prefectural reformation is passed), by Kushiro Shinbun, June 29, 2008 The change should have taken effect in April 2009. However, it was impossible to make the reform on time. The Public Offices Election Act and the Election Law were not amended on April 1, 2009, but the amendment the Public Offices Election Act was passed in the prefectural assembly on March 31, 2009, and took effect from April 1, 2010. ===List of subprefecture bureaus=== *[[:en:Sorachi General Subprefectural Bureau|Sorachi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (空知総合振興局), Iwamizawa, covering Ishikari and Sorachi areas. ({{ill|Horokanai, Hokkaido|en|Horokanai, Hokkaido|WD=Q1355616|lt=Horokanai|1=Horokanai, Hokkaido}} belongs to Kamikawa Subprefectural Bureau.) **[[:en:Ishikari Subprefectural Bureau|Ishikari Subprefectural Bureau]] (石狩振興局), Sapporo, covering Ishikari area *[[:en:Shiribeshi General Subprefectural Bureau|Shiribeshi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (後志総合振興局), Kutchan, covering Shiribeshi area *[[:en:Iburi General Subprefectural Bureau|Iburi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (胆振総合振興局), Muroran, covering Iburi and Hidaka areas **[[:en:Hidaka Subprefectural Bureau|Hidaka Subprefectural Bureau]] (日高振興局), Urakawa, covering Hidaka area *[[:en:Oshima General Subprefectural Bureau|Oshima General Subprefectural Bureau]] (渡島総合振興局), Hakodate, covering Oshima and Hiyama areas **[[:en:Hiyama Subprefectural Bureau|Hiyama Subprefectural Bureau]] (檜山振興局), Esashi, covering Hiyama area *[[:en:Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau|Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau]] (上川総合振興局), Asahikawa, covering Kamikawa and Rumoi areas **[[:en:Rumoi Subprefectural Bureau|Rumoi Subprefectural Bureau]] (留萌振興局), Rumoi, covering Rumoi area. ({{ill|Horonobe, Hokkaido|en|Horonobe, Hokkaido|WD=Q955532|lt=Horonobe|1=Horonobe, Hokkaido}} belongs to Soya Subprefectural Bureau.) *[[:en:Sōya General Subprefectural Bureau|Sōya General Subprefectural Bureau]] (宗谷総合振興局), Wakkanai, covering Sōya area *[[:en:Okhotsk General Subprefectural Bureau|Okhotsk General Subprefectural Bureau]] (オホーツク総合振興局), Abashiri, covering Abashiri area *[[:en:Tokachi General Subprefectural Bureau|Tokachi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (十勝総合振興局), Obihiro, covering Tokachi area *[[:en:Kushiro General Subprefectural Bureau|Kushiro General Subprefectural Bureau]] (釧路総合振興局), Kushiro, covering Kushiro and Nemuro areas **[[:en:Nemuro Subprefectural Bureau|Nemuro Subprefectural Bureau]] (根室振興局), Nemuro, covering Nemuro area ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/gyosei/shicho/index.htm Official websites] of the subprefectural bureaus {{in lang|ja}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20130903112433/http://pucchi.net/hokkaido/gappei/area_info.php The map of the new development bureaus], from Pucchi Guide {{in lang|ja}} {{Hokkaido}} [[en:Subprefectures of Hokkaido]] [[ca:Subprefectures de Hokkaido]] [[es:Subprefecturas de Hokkaidō]] [[id:Subprefektur di Hokkaido]] [[ilo:Dagiti subprepektura ti Hokkaido]] [[lt:Hokaido subprefektūros]] [[nl:Subprefecturen van Hokkaido]] [[ru:Округа Хоккайдо]] [[th:กิ่งจังหวัดของฮกไกโด]] [[tl:Mga subprepektura ng Hokkaidō]] {{wikidata link|Q2330190}} [[Category:Subprefectures in Hokkaido| ]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed parentheses]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

({{ill|Kuril Islands dispute|en|Kuril Islands dispute|WD=Q389717|lt=Northern Territories|1=Kuril Islands dispute}}), currently administered by Russia,
belong to Nemuro Subprefecture divided into six villages. However, the table above excludes these islands' data. |} ==Reorganisation== The prefectural government of Hokkaido planned to reorganise the current fourteen subprefectures into nine sub-prefectural bureaus. Five subprefectures, namely Hidaka, Hiyama, Ishikari, Nemuro, and Rumoi were subject to be cut down. The capital municipalities of these subprefectures opposed the plan, but on June 28, 2008, the prefectural council passed the ordinance of the reorganization.{{in lang|ja}} [http://www.namara-hokkaido.net/topics/news/news.php?id=15017 釧路支庁が道東総合振興局に、道の支庁改革条例成立] (Kushiro Subprefecture will be Dōtō General Development Bureau, the ordinance for the sub-prefectural reformation is passed), by Kushiro Shinbun, June 29, 2008 The change should have taken effect in April 2009. However, it was impossible to make the reform on time. The Public Offices Election Act and the Election Law were not amended on April 1, 2009, but the amendment the Public Offices Election Act was passed in the prefectural assembly on March 31, 2009, and took effect from April 1, 2010. ===List of subprefecture bureaus=== *[[:en:Sorachi General Subprefectural Bureau|Sorachi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (空知総合振興局), Iwamizawa, covering Ishikari and Sorachi areas. ({{ill|Horokanai, Hokkaido|en|Horokanai, Hokkaido|WD=Q1355616|lt=Horokanai|1=Horokanai, Hokkaido}} belongs to Kamikawa Subprefectural Bureau.) **[[:en:Ishikari Subprefectural Bureau|Ishikari Subprefectural Bureau]] (石狩振興局), Sapporo, covering Ishikari area *[[:en:Shiribeshi General Subprefectural Bureau|Shiribeshi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (後志総合振興局), Kutchan, covering Shiribeshi area *[[:en:Iburi General Subprefectural Bureau|Iburi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (胆振総合振興局), Muroran, covering Iburi and Hidaka areas **[[:en:Hidaka Subprefectural Bureau|Hidaka Subprefectural Bureau]] (日高振興局), Urakawa, covering Hidaka area *[[:en:Oshima General Subprefectural Bureau|Oshima General Subprefectural Bureau]] (渡島総合振興局), Hakodate, covering Oshima and Hiyama areas **[[:en:Hiyama Subprefectural Bureau|Hiyama Subprefectural Bureau]] (檜山振興局), Esashi, covering Hiyama area *[[:en:Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau|Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau]] (上川総合振興局), Asahikawa, covering Kamikawa and Rumoi areas **[[:en:Rumoi Subprefectural Bureau|Rumoi Subprefectural Bureau]] (留萌振興局), Rumoi, covering Rumoi area. ({{ill|Horonobe, Hokkaido|en|Horonobe, Hokkaido|WD=Q955532|lt=Horonobe|1=Horonobe, Hokkaido}} belongs to Soya Subprefectural Bureau.) *[[:en:Sōya General Subprefectural Bureau|Sōya General Subprefectural Bureau]] (宗谷総合振興局), Wakkanai, covering Sōya area *[[:en:Okhotsk General Subprefectural Bureau|Okhotsk General Subprefectural Bureau]] (オホーツク総合振興局), Abashiri, covering Abashiri area *[[:en:Tokachi General Subprefectural Bureau|Tokachi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (十勝総合振興局), Obihiro, covering Tokachi area *[[:en:Kushiro General Subprefectural Bureau|Kushiro General Subprefectural Bureau]] (釧路総合振興局), Kushiro, covering Kushiro and Nemuro areas **[[:en:Nemuro Subprefectural Bureau|Nemuro Subprefectural Bureau]] (根室振興局), Nemuro, covering Nemuro area ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/gyosei/shicho/index.htm Official websites] of the subprefectural bureaus {{in lang|ja}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20130903112433/http://pucchi.net/hokkaido/gappei/area_info.php The map of the new development bureaus], from Pucchi Guide {{in lang|ja}} {{Hokkaido}} [[en:Subprefectures of Hokkaido]] [[ca:Subprefectures de Hokkaido]] [[es:Subprefecturas de Hokkaidō]] [[id:Subprefektur di Hokkaido]] [[ilo:Dagiti subprepektura ti Hokkaido]] [[lt:Hokaido subprefektūros]] [[nl:Subprefecturen van Hokkaido]] [[ru:Округа Хоккайдо]] [[th:กิ่งจังหวัดของฮกไกโด]] [[tl:Mga subprepektura ng Hokkaidō]] {{wikidata link|Q2330190}} [[Category:Subprefectures in Hokkaido| ]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

revision.text:
[[ca:Subprefectures de Hokkaido]][[en:Subprefectures of Hokkaido]][[es:Subprefecturas de Hokkaidō]][[id:Subprefektur di Hokkaido]][[ilo:Dagiti subprepektura ti Hokkaido]][[lt:Hokaido subprefektūros]][[nl:Subprefecturen van Hokkaido]][[ru:Округа Хоккайдо]][[th:กิ่งจังหวัดของฮกไกโด]][[tl:Mga subprepektura ng Hokkaidō]] [[ca:Subprefectures de Hokkaido]][[en:Subprefectures of Hokkaido]][[es:Subprefecturas de Hokkaidō]][[id:Subprefektur di Hokkaido]][[ilo:Dagiti subprepektura ti Hokkaido]][[lt:Hokaido subprefektūros]][[nl:Subprefecturen van Hokkaido]][[ru:Округа Хоккайдо]][[th:กิ่งจังหวัดของฮกไกโด]][[tl:Mga subprepektura ng Hokkaidō]] [[ca:Subprefectures de Hokkaido]][[en:Subprefectures of Hokkaido]][[es:Subprefecturas de Hokkaidō]][[id:Subprefektur di Hokkaido]][[ilo:Dagiti subprepektura ti Hokkaido]][[lt:Hokaido subprefektūros]][[nl:Subprefecturen van Hokkaido]][[ru:Округа Хоккайдо]][[th:กิ่งจังหวัดของฮกไกโด]][[tl:Mga subprepektura ng Hokkaidō]] {{short description|List of subprefectures in Hokkaido, Japan}} {{One source|date=October 2018}} [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaido Prefecture]] had 14 branch offices called 支庁 (shichō) in {{ill|Japanese language|en|Japanese language|WD=Q5287|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese language}}, which is often translated in English as '''subprefectures'''. Normally, a subprefecture consists of a few to a dozen cities, towns, and/or villages. From April 2010, Hokkaido has nine General Subprefecture Bureaus (総合振興局, sōgō-shinkō-kyoku, literally "Comprehensive Promotion Bureau") and five Subprefecture Bureaus (振興局, shinkō-kyoku, literally "Promotion Bureau")). For historical reasons, some older people in Hokkaido use the subprefecture name suffixed by ''-kannai'' in their address. ==History== {| |width="50px"| 1897 || Nineteen ''shichō'' were placed under Hokkaido Agency (an agency of the national government): {{ill|Sapporo|en|Sapporo|WD=Q37951|lt=Sapporo|1=Sapporo}}, {{ill|Hakodate|en|Hakodate|WD=Q26418|lt=Hakodate|1=Hakodate}}, Kameda, Matsumae, Hiyama, Suttsu, Iwanai, Otaru, Sorachi, Kamikawa, Mashike, Souya, Abashiri, Muroran, Urakawa, Kushiro, Kasai, Nemuro, and Shana. |- | 1899 || Sapporo-''ku'', Hakodate-''ku'', and Otaru-''ku'' were established as municipalities independent of ''shichō''. Sapporo-''shichō'' and Otaru-''shichō'' were continued with remaining area, but Hakodate-''shichō'' was dissolved. Previous Kameda-''shichō'' was renamed Hakodate-''shichō''. |- | 1903 || Matsumae-''shichō'' was merged with Hakodate-''shichō''. Shana-''shichō'' was merged with Nemuro-''shichō''. |- | 1910 || Suttsu-''shichō'', Iwanai-''shichō'', and Otaru-''shichō'' were merged to form Shiribeshi-''shichō''. |- | 1914 || Asahikawa-''ku'' was established as a municipality independent of Kamikawa-''shichō''. Mashike-''shichō'' was renamed as Rumoi-''shichō''. |- | 1918 || Muroran-''ku'' was established as a municipality independent of Muroran-''shichō''. |- | 1920 || Kushiro-''ku'' was established as a municipality independent of Kushiro-''shichō''. |- | 1922 || The six ''ku'' were restated as ''shi'' (cities). Sapporo-''shichō'' was renamed Ishikari-''shichō''. Hakodate-''shichō'' was renamed Oshima-''shichō''. Muroran-''shichō'' was renamed Iburi-''shichō''. |- | 1932 || Urakawa-''shichō'' was renamed Hidaka-''shichō''. Kasai-''shichō'' was renamed Tokachi-''shichō''. |- | 1947 || Hokkaido Agency was abolished and [[:en:Hokkaidō|Hokkaido Prefecture]] was established. Hokkaido Prefecture took over ''shichō''. |} == List of subprefectures == {{further|Former Provinces of Hokkaido}} {{see also|List of mergers in Hokkaido}} {| class="wikitable sortable" |- | colspan="11" style="text-align: center; padding-left: 18%" |{{Image label begin|image=Subprefectures of Hokkaido.svg|width=500}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.38|y=0.40|text='''1'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.32|y=0.45|text='''a'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.23|y=0.48|text='''2'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.28|y=0.55|text='''3'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.45|y=0.55|text='''b'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.21|y=0.65|text='''4'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.1|y=0.6|text='''c'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.47|y=0.32|text='''5'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.37|y=0.29|text='''d'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.4|y=0.1|text='''6'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.60|y=0.32|text='''7'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.55|y=0.50|text='''8'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.69|y=0.46|text='''9'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.76|y=0.37|text='''e'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=0.87|y=0.27|text='''(disputed)'''}} {{Image label|scale=|x=1.00|y=0.1|text='''(disputed)'''}} {{Image label end}} |- ! colspan="2" rowspan="2" |{{ill|Subprefectures of Japan|en|Subprefectures of Japan|WD=Q850450|lt=Subprefecture|1=Subprefectures of Japan}} ! rowspan="2" |Japanese ! rowspan="2" |Capital ! rowspan="2" |Largest municipality ! rowspan="2" |Pop.

Five subprefectures, namely Hidaka, Hiyama, Ishikari, Nemuro, and Rumoi were subject to be cut down. The capital municipalities of these subprefectures opposed the plan, but on June 28, 2008, the prefectural council passed the ordinance of the reorganization.{{in lang|ja}} [http://www.namara-hokkaido.net/topics/news/news.php?id=15017 釧路支庁が道東総合振興局に、道の支庁改革条例成立] (Kushiro Subprefecture will be Dōtō General Development Bureau, the ordinance for the sub-prefectural reformation is passed), by Kushiro Shinbun, June 29, 2008 The change should have taken effect in April 2009. However, it was impossible to make the reform on time. The Public Offices Election Act and the Election Law were not amended on April 1, 2009, but the amendment the Public Offices Election Act was passed in the prefectural assembly on March 31, 2009, and took effect from April 1, 2010. ===List of subprefecture bureaus=== *[[:en:Sorachi General Subprefectural Bureau|Sorachi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (空知総合振興局), Iwamizawa, covering Ishikari and Sorachi areas. ({{ill|Horokanai, Hokkaido|en|Horokanai, Hokkaido|WD=Q1355616|lt=Horokanai|1=Horokanai, Hokkaido}} belongs to Kamikawa Subprefectural Bureau.) **[[:en:Ishikari Subprefectural Bureau|Ishikari Subprefectural Bureau]] (石狩振興局), Sapporo, covering Ishikari area *[[:en:Shiribeshi General Subprefectural Bureau|Shiribeshi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (後志総合振興局), Kutchan, covering Shiribeshi area *[[:en:Iburi General Subprefectural Bureau|Iburi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (胆振総合振興局), Muroran, covering Iburi and Hidaka areas **[[:en:Hidaka Subprefectural Bureau|Hidaka Subprefectural Bureau]] (日高振興局), Urakawa, covering Hidaka area *[[:en:Oshima General Subprefectural Bureau|Oshima General Subprefectural Bureau]] (渡島総合振興局), Hakodate, covering Oshima and Hiyama areas **[[:en:Hiyama Subprefectural Bureau|Hiyama Subprefectural Bureau]] (檜山振興局), Esashi, covering Hiyama area *[[:en:Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau|Kamikawa General Subprefectural Bureau]] (上川総合振興局), Asahikawa, covering Kamikawa and Rumoi areas **[[:en:Rumoi Subprefectural Bureau|Rumoi Subprefectural Bureau]] (留萌振興局), Rumoi, covering Rumoi area. ({{ill|Horonobe, Hokkaido|en|Horonobe, Hokkaido|WD=Q955532|lt=Horonobe|1=Horonobe, Hokkaido}} belongs to Soya Subprefectural Bureau.) *[[:en:Sōya General Subprefectural Bureau|Sōya General Subprefectural Bureau]] (宗谷総合振興局), Wakkanai, covering Sōya area *[[:en:Okhotsk General Subprefectural Bureau|Okhotsk General Subprefectural Bureau]] (オホーツク総合振興局), Abashiri, covering Abashiri area *[[:en:Tokachi General Subprefectural Bureau|Tokachi General Subprefectural Bureau]] (十勝総合振興局), Obihiro, covering Tokachi area *[[:en:Kushiro General Subprefectural Bureau|Kushiro General Subprefectural Bureau]] (釧路総合振興局), Kushiro, covering Kushiro and Nemuro areas **[[:en:Nemuro Subprefectural Bureau|Nemuro Subprefectural Bureau]] (根室振興局), Nemuro, covering Nemuro area ==References== {{reflist}} ==External links== * [http://www.pref.hokkaido.lg.jp/gyosei/shicho/index.htm Official websites] of the subprefectural bureaus {{in lang|ja}} * [https://web.archive.org/web/20130903112433/http://pucchi.net/hokkaido/gappei/area_info.php The map of the new development bureaus], from Pucchi Guide {{in lang|ja}} {{Hokkaido}} [[en:Subprefectures of Hokkaido]] [[ca:Subprefectures de Hokkaido]] [[es:Subprefecturas de Hokkaidō]] [[id:Subprefektur di Hokkaido]] [[ilo:Dagiti subprepektura ti Hokkaido]] [[lt:Hokaido subprefektūros]] [[nl:Subprefecturen van Hokkaido]] [[ru:Округа Хоккайдо]] [[th:กิ่งจังหวัดของฮกไกโด]] [[tl:Mga subprepektura ng Hokkaidō]] {{wikidata link|Q2330190}} [[Category:Subprefectures in Hokkaido| ]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
